nodes.title: What does research data loss cost institutions and science?
Is research data loss architectural or operational?

What is the downstream signature of research data loss in the published record?
How is the scholarly record itself decaying through reference rot?

What architecture produces verification evidence as a byproduct of operation?
What loads the conditions under which the latent §5 liability surfaces as realized cost?
What costs accrue continuously regardless of whether the latent liability is surfaced?

Is the asymmetry between liability and prevention cost large enough to bind without timing assumptions?
Under what architectural conditions does research data survive long horizons?
How does personnel turnover function as a preservation event for research data?
How does grant termination convert to data-maintenance termination?
How do platforms discontinue, restrict access, or become unaffordable on timelines researchers do not control?
What structural property is shared across the documented loss mechanisms?
Why is the compliance gap structural rather than behavioral?
Does content addressing change the evidentiary status of "my data is lost" misconduct claims?

What does Tier 2 coordinated preservation cost, and what conditions make it sustainable?
What pattern distinguishes Tier 2 consortia that survive from those that don't?
What is the documented return on investment for research data infrastructure across studies?
How does sensitive data participate in Tier 3 architecture without exposure?
What does open access multiply about preserved-data ROI?
What does Tier 1 hosted storage cost per dataset, and what fails it?

What is the demonstrated ceiling of coordinated preservation, and where does it stop?
When do ordinary physical and technical events become preservation events?
What three operational forms does Tier 2 fragility take?
What architecture would close the coverage gap to the 73-93% sitting outside Tier 2?
What should research institutions do to deploy resilient data architecture?
What should funders do to align incentives with verifiable preservation?
What should individual researchers do as standard laboratory practice?
What should the working group do to lower adoption costs of the other recommendations?

Research data loss is architectural, not operational

Tier 2 economics produce the surviving-consortia pattern
ROI on research data infrastructure is positive in every documented study, ranging 5x to 800x

Tier 2 coordinated preservation works but does not extend to most research
Three architectural properties jointly determine long-horizon survival

Tier 1 is one organizational decision away from the same outcome as Tier 0
